headline: Iran Closes Hormuz Strait as Israel Strikes Hezbollah, U.S. Envoy Heads to Switzerland
subheadline: The IRGC shut Hormuz, citing Israeli attacks; Israel struck 300 Hezbollah sites after 50 rockets, while U.S. envoy Vance heads to Switzerland for ceasefire talks.
lede: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first step in retaliation for Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon and alleged U.S. cease‑fire violations. Overnight the Israeli Defence Forces launched airstrikes on dozens of Hezbollah positions in southern Lebanon, responding to an estimated 50 projectiles fired at Israeli forces. New U.S. diplomatic envoy Vance and Iran's delegation departed for Switzerland to discuss the Lebanon ceasefire and nuclear issues. Iranian officials warned that further missile launches toward Israel could occur if aggression against Lebanon continues.

sections.title: Ukraine Pushes Back Amid Escalating Air Strikes
sections.body: Russian Su‑34 jets struck UAV control sites in Dobropolye, Kucherov Yar and Nikolaevka within the Donetsk People's Republic. Ukraine responded with a wave of Shahed drones, eight over Izmail and one over northern Rivne, while a ballistic missile was detected over Poltava. Ukrainian forces hit occupied Crimea, igniting fires at the Tavriya thermal plant, an Odessa‑region gas station and an oil‑gas depot. Russian KAB bombardments on Zaporizhzhia caused four deaths, six injuries and damage to at least twenty houses.

sections.title: Iran Tightens Grip, Lebanon Front Ignites
sections.body: The IRGC announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessel traffic, citing Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon and U.S. cease‑fire breaches. The IDF confirmed overnight airstrikes on dozens of Hezbollah sites, saying they responded to roughly 50 projectiles launched at Israeli forces. Iran's foreign ministry demanded that Israel halt its attacks on Lebanon, while Iranian media warned missiles could be launched toward Israel if aggression continues. Iran's negotiating team, led by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, departed for Switzerland for technical talks with the United States.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The simultaneous closure of Hormuz and intensified strikes in Lebanon signal a coordinated escalation by Iran and Israel, expanding the conflict to strategic maritime routes. Ukraine's broadened use of Shahed drones and strikes on Crimea, coupled with Russian high‑speed air attacks, indicate a deepening of the aerial battle space on both fronts.
